name: <AGENT_NAME>
_comment_capabilities: The two OPTIONAL launch capabilities. The hub hardcodes no producer name or path — it executes only what you declare here, and only if `dir` resolves to a direct sibling of the hub repo. Delete whichever you do not implement; a producer that only writes markdown by hand needs neither. They are deliberately SEPARATE: proposing reads the corpus and writes markdown into the human gate and costs nothing, while rendering spends image-API credits per frame — so the free, unattended trigger must never be gated on, or grantable by, the paid one.

config_schema.title: <AGENT_NAME> config
config_schema.description: Tunable knobs for this producer (the hub contract §10.3). The hub stores the live values at config/agents/<AGENT_NAME>.json and the Dashboard renders a form from this schema. Fill in / edit per agent.

platform.description: Which platform corpus/analysis to produce against.

variants_per_run.description: How many distinct outputs to generate per run.

min_score.description: Only build from exemplars/blueprints at or above this virality score.

eval_threshold.description: Self-eval score an output must reach before it is published (§10.2).
